storyInfo.storyDescription: As a user, I want to create, read, update, and delete tasks so that I can manage my work items effectively. Tasks should support title, description, priority (critical/high/medium/low), due date, tags, and assignee fields.
storyInfo.businessValue: Core task management is the foundation of the entire TaskFlow Pro platform. Without reliable CRUD operations, no other features (AI prioritization, filtering, sync) can function.
storyInfo.technicalContext: Tasks are stored in PostgreSQL with a REST API. Real-time updates are broadcast via WebSocket. Task validation occurs at both API and service layers.

storySummary.iWant: to create, read, update, and delete tasks with rich metadata (title, description, priority, due date, tags, assignee)
storySummary.soThat: I can effectively track and manage my work items in a structured way

acceptanceCriteria.title: User can create a task with all supported fields
acceptanceCriteria.given: An authenticated user on the task creation form
acceptanceCriteria.when: The user fills in title, description, priority, due date, and tags then submits
acceptanceCriteria.then: A new task is created with a unique ID and appears in the task list

acceptanceCriteria.title: User can update any field of an existing task
acceptanceCriteria.given: An existing task displayed in the task detail view
acceptanceCriteria.when: The user modifies the title and changes the priority
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task is updated immediately and the updatedAt timestamp changes

acceptanceCriteria.title: User can delete a task with confirmation
acceptanceCriteria.given: An existing task in the task list
acceptanceCriteria.when: The user clicks delete and confirms the action
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task is permanently removed and no longer appears in any view

acceptanceCriteria.title: Task validation prevents invalid data
acceptanceCriteria.given: A user attempting to create or update a task
acceptanceCriteria.when: The user submits with an empty title or invalid due date
acceptanceCriteria.then: A clear validation error is shown and the task is not saved

integration.name: POST /api/tasks creates task with all fields
integration.description: Integration test verifying task creation via API with all supported fields returns 201 and persisted task
given: An authenticated user with a valid JWT token
when: POST /api/tasks is called with title, description, priority='high', dueDate='2025-10-15', tags=['feature','urgent']
then: Response body contains the created task with a generated UUID
The task exists in the database with all fields matching the request

integration.name: PATCH /api/tasks/:id updates task fields
integration.description: Integration test verifying partial task update via API correctly persists changes and updates timestamp
given: An existing task with id='task-001' and title='Original Title'
when: PATCH /api/tasks/task-001 is called with { title: 'Updated Title', priority: 'critical' }
then: Response body shows updated title and priority
updatedAt timestamp is newer than createdAt

integration.name: DELETE /api/tasks/:id removes task permanently
integration.description: Integration test verifying task deletion returns 204 and task is no longer retrievable

unit.name: TaskValidator rejects empty title
unit.description: Unit test verifying task validation throws ValidationError for empty title
given: A task creation request with title=''
then: A ValidationError is thrown with message 'Title is required'

e2e.name: User creates task through UI
e2e.description: E2E test verifying the complete task creation flow from clicking New Task to seeing the task in the list
given: The user is logged in and on the task board page
then: The task appears in the task list
The task card shows title 'E2E Test Task' and priority badge 'high'

testScenarios.name: Create task with all fields populated
given: An authenticated user on the task board
when: The user opens the task creation form
Fills in all fields: title, description, priority, due date, tags, assignee
then: A new task is created with all fields persisted
The task appears in the correct priority column
A WebSocket event notifies other clients

testScenarios.name: Validation error on empty title submission
given: An authenticated user on the task creation form
when: The user leaves the title field empty and clicks Create
then: A validation error message 'Title is required' is displayed
The form remains open for correction

requiredDataTestIds.purpose: Trigger task creation form - used in E2E tests to open the new task dialog
Title input field for task creation and editing
Delete task button with confirmation dialog
Individual task card in the task list for selecting and interacting with tasks

implementationChecklist.item: Write failing integration tests for all CRUD endpoints (POST, GET, PATCH, DELETE)
implementationChecklist.notes: 4 integration tests created, all initially failing

implementationChecklist.item: Write failing unit tests for TaskValidator edge cases
Implement Task model and database migration

implementationChecklist.notes: REST endpoints with proper HTTP status codes

implementationChecklist.item: Refactor: extract validation into separate TaskValidator class
Write E2E tests for complete user journey
implementationChecklist.notes: 2 E2E tests covering create and delete flows

implementationChecklist.item: Add data-testid attributes to all interactive elements
Final test run and coverage verification
implementationChecklist.notes: All 12 tests passing, 94% coverage for task module

red.description: Wrote integration test for POST /api/tasks that expects 201 response with created task containing all fields. Test fails because API endpoint doesn't exist yet.
red.testName: POST /api/tasks creates task with all fields
red.failureMessage: Error: connect ECONNREFUSED - POST /api/tasks returned network error (no server)

green.description: Implemented Task model, database migration, TaskService.create(), and POST /api/tasks endpoint. Test now passes with correct 201 response and all fields persisted.

refactor.description: Extracted input validation from TaskController into dedicated TaskValidator class. Added TypeScript interfaces for Task and CreateTaskDTO. Simplified controller to single-responsibility.

iterations.targetAC: AC-1.1.2 + AC-1.1.3 - Task update and delete

red.description: Wrote integration tests for PATCH /api/tasks/:id and DELETE /api/tasks/:id. Both fail because endpoints don't exist yet.
red.failureMessage: Error: PATCH /api/tasks/task-001 returned 404 - endpoint not found

green.description: Implemented TaskService.update() and TaskService.delete() methods, added PATCH and DELETE routes. All tests now pass.

refactor.description: Added optimistic locking via version field to prevent concurrent update conflicts. Extracted route registration to separate file.

red.description: Wrote 6 unit tests for TaskValidator covering empty title, title too long, invalid date, invalid priority, XSS in description, and duplicate tags. All fail because TaskValidator doesn't handle these cases.
red.failureMessage: Expected ValidationError to be thrown but no error was thrown

green.description: Added validation rules to TaskValidator for all 6 cases. All unit tests pass. Integration tests also updated to verify 422 responses for invalid input.

refactor.description: Refactored validation to use a fluent builder pattern for composable validation rules. Added custom error messages with field-level error details in 422 response.
improvements: Field-level error details in API response

nextSteps: Monitor test stability in CI over next 5 runs
Add performance benchmarks for CRUD operations
